subject: Geography
title: Urban Sprawl
description: A micro-lesson that makes Urban Sprawl usable.

hook.text: Los Angeles sprawls across 12,500 square km—nearly the size of Montenegro.

buildup.text: Cheap cars and highways let cities spread outward instead of building upward.

discovery.text: Sprawl devours farmland, forests, and wetlands as suburbs expand endlessly.

twist.text: Commuters in sprawling cities spend years of their lives sitting in traffic.

climax.text: Sprawl is now seen as a design failure—expensive, polluting, and isolating.

punchline.text: Spreading out sounded like freedom. It became a trap.

quiz.question: What drives urban sprawl?
options: Cheap cars and highways encouraging outward growth
